methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $182M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Standard Nuclear, founded by Kurt Terrani, is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $182M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a frontier-tech sector with fragmented comparable company data: three public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private competitors including Fuse Energy, X-Energy, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($182M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A-to-later-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $364M. This method reflects market dynamics in which capital deployed correlates with valuation step-ups at subsequent funding rounds. Peer-median analysis is not applicable: while four same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, similar-tier valuation) did not yield the minimum three datapoints required for statistically meaningful median comparison. A broader peer median would introduce unacceptable methodological bias.

The triangulated valuation range is $309M (low) to $419M (high), with a central estimate of $364M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty stemming from limited comparable data density in nuclear energy—a nascent, capital-intensive sector where valuations remain highly dependent on technology risk, regulatory trajectory, and capital availability. Key sensitivities include deployment timelines, project financing success, and near-term commodity price movements affecting comparables valuations.
